title: Future perfect and future continuous
explanation:
Future perfect and future continuous

Future perfect: will have + past participle. Use it to say something will be completed before a future time.

Future continuous: will be + verb-ing. Use it for actions in progress at a future moment.

questions.text: By next June, I [*will have finished|will finish|finish] my degree.
questions.feedback: Future perfect for completion before a point in the future.

questions.text: This time tomorrow, we [*will be flying|will fly|are flying] over the Atlantic.
questions.feedback: Future continuous for an action in progress.

questions.text: By the end of the week, they [*will have moved|will move|move] into the new office.
At 9 p.m., she [*will be giving|will give|gives] her presentation.
questions.feedback: Action in progress at a future time.

questions.text: I [*will have read|will be reading|read] the report by the time you arrive.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: In two years, he ____ in the company for a decade. | *will have worked | will work | will be worked
questions.feedback: Future perfect with duration up to a future point.

questions.text: Do not call at eight - we ____ dinner then. | *will be having | will have | have
By tomorrow morning, the technicians ____ the problem. | *will have solved | solve | will be solving
At this time next week, I ____ on a beach. | *will be lying | lie | will have lain
questions.feedback: Action in progress in the future.

questions.text: By 2030, scientists [*will have developed|develop|will be developed] new solutions to this problem.
When you get home, I [*will be sleeping|sleep|will have slept] probably .
questions.feedback: Likely in progress at that moment.

questions.text: They [*will have known|know|will be knowing] each other for twenty years next month.
At noon tomorrow, the students [*will be taking|take|will have taken] their exam.
questions.feedback: Action in progress at a future moment.

questions.text: By the time the guests arrive, we [*will have prepared|prepare|will be preparing] everything.
